tokens.range: destitute
a heap of rubbish or filth
properly, dangling, i.e. (by implication) weak or thin
dust (as powdered or gray); hence, clay, earth, mud
to rise (in various applications, literal, figurative, intensive and causative)
to be high actively, to rise or raise (in various applications, literally or figuratively)

gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a supe
to soar, i.e. be lofty; figuratively, to be haughty

tokens.text: Who is like unto the LORD
tokens.range: Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
properly, to sit down (specifically as judge. in ambush, in quiet); by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry

flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.
יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.

5.struct: contains a main verb; ⚑ theologically contested vocabulary

tokens.range: a mother (as the bond of the family); in a wide sense (both literally and figuratively (like father))
a house (in the greatest variation of applications, especially family, etc.)
a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense (of literal and figurative relationship, including grandson, subject, nation, quality or condition, etc., (like father or brother), etc.)
to be clear (orig. of sound, but usually of color); to shine; hence, to make ashow, to boast; and thus to be (clamorously) foolish; to rave; causatively, to celebrate; also to stultify

properly, to sit down (specifically as judge. in ambush, in quiet); by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry
sterile (as if extirpated in the generative organs)
blithe or gleeful
the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
to see, literally or figuratively (in numerous applications, direct and implied, transitive, intransitive and causative)

tokens.text: the things that are in heaven
tokens.range: the sky (as aloft; the dual perhaps alluding to the visible arch in which the clouds move, as well as to the higher ether where the celestial bodies revolve)
to depress or sink (expectation figuratively, to humiliate, intransitive or transitive)
to kneel; by implication to bless God (as an act of adoration), and (vice-versa) man (as a benefit); also (by euphemism) to curse (God or the king, as treason)
Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
as far (or long, or much) as, whether of space (even unto) or time (during, while, until) or degree (equally with)
properly, concealed, i.e. the vanishing point; generally, time out of mind (past or future), i.e. (practically) eternity; frequentatively, adverbial (especially with prepositional prefix) always
at this time, whether adverb, conjunction or expletive
an appellation, as amark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character

flags.note: יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.
עוֹלָם — "eternal" / "everlasting" / "age-long." Like Greek αἰώνιος, debate centers on whether this is absolute timelessness or the span of an era.
